questions.promptHtml: Your organization deploys a hub-spoke topology with a hub VNet (10.0.0.0/16) and 20 spoke VNets (10.1.0.0/16 through 10.20.0.0/16). Each spoke needs to communicate with shared services in the hub and with each other. Which routing configuration enables this full-mesh communication?

options.label: Spoke-to-spoke VNet peering without hub involvement
Hub VNet peering with gateway transit and Allow Forwarded Traffic enabled
No peering configuration needed

questions.correctLabel: Hub VNet peering with gateway transit and Allow Forwarded Traffic enabled
questions.extraHtml:
Hub-spoke topology with gateway transit allows spokes to communicate through hub using UDRs or virtual network gateway transit. Allow Forwarded Traffic permits spoke-to-spoke via hub. Direct spoke-to-spoke peering creates management complexity with 190 peerings. No peering prevents communication.

Reference: Hub-spoke network topology

questions.promptHtml: A VNet requires IP address space for 500 VMs, 50 Azure Firewall instances, 10 Application Gateways, and Azure Bastion. VMs need /24 subnet, firewalls need /26, Application Gateways need /27, and Bastion needs /26. Which VNet CIDR provides sufficient capacity with minimal waste?

options.label: /16 providing 65,536 addresses
/20 providing 4,096 addresses
/24 providing 256 addresses

questions.correctLabel: /20 providing 4,096 addresses
questions.extraHtml:
/20 provides 4,096 IPs: VMs need 512 (/23 or two /24s), Firewalls 64 (/26), App GW 128 (4x /27 minimum), Bastion 64 (/26) = ~768 + Azure reserved ~5% = under 1000, fitting in /20. /16 wastes 60K+ addresses. /24 insufficient for requirements.

Reference: VNet sizing

questions.promptHtml: Your organization owns public IP range 203.0.113.0/24 and wants to use it in Azure instead of Azure-provided public IPs for regulatory compliance. Which Azure feature enables bringing your own IP addresses?

options.label: Standard public IP addresses without custom range
Custom public IP address prefix (BYOIP) with range attestation
Private IP addresses only

questions.correctLabel: Custom public IP address prefix (BYOIP) with range attestation
questions.extraHtml:
Custom IP prefix (BYOIP) allows bringing customer-owned public IP ranges to Azure after ownership verification. Standard Azure public IPs use Microsoft-owned ranges. Private IPs don't provide public internet connectivity required.

Reference: Bring your own IP

questions.promptHtml: A multi-region application requires DNS resolution where users connect to the nearest healthy region. The solution must automatically remove failed regions from DNS within 30 seconds. Which Azure DNS configuration achieves this?

options.label: Azure DNS public zone with static A records
Azure Traffic Manager with performance routing and health probes
Azure Private DNS without monitoring

questions.correctLabel: Azure Traffic Manager with performance routing and health probes
questions.extraHtml:
Traffic Manager provides DNS-based global traffic distribution with performance routing directing users to nearest region and health probes automatically removing unhealthy endpoints. Static DNS lacks health monitoring. Private DNS doesn't support public internet routing.

Reference: Traffic Manager routing

questions.promptHtml: Your solution requires private DNS resolution between Azure VNets and on-premises networks with conditional forwarding for specific domain suffixes. Which Azure DNS solution provides this hybrid DNS capability?

options.label: Azure public DNS zones
Azure DNS Private Resolver with forwarding rules
On-premises DNS only

questions.correctLabel: Azure DNS Private Resolver with forwarding rules
questions.extraHtml:
Azure DNS Private Resolver enables centralized DNS resolution with conditional forwarding between Azure and on-premises, supporting private DNS zones and custom forwarding rules. Public zones don't resolve private names. On-premises-only DNS can't resolve Azure private DNS zones.

Reference: Azure DNS Private Resolver

questions.promptHtml: A VNet requires a subnet delegated to Azure SQL Managed Instance. The managed instance needs exclusive control over the subnet. Which subnet configuration parameter enables this service delegation?

options.label: No delegation, shared subnet with other resources
Subnet delegation to Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances service
Network security group rules only

questions.correctLabel: Subnet delegation to Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances service
questions.extraHtml:
Subnet delegation grants exclusive control to specific Azure services requiring network policy management. SQL Managed Instance requires delegated subnet. Shared subnets don't provide service-required network control. NSG rules alone don't delegate subnet control.

Reference: Subnet delegation

questions.promptHtml: Your organization deploys Azure Kubernetes Service and Azure App Service requiring dedicated subnets in the same VNet. Both services need outbound internet access. Which subnet design provides proper isolation and connectivity?

options.label: Single shared subnet for all services
Dedicated subnets per service with shared NAT Gateway or Azure Firewall
No internet connectivity configuration

questions.correctLabel: Dedicated subnets per service with shared NAT Gateway or Azure Firewall
questions.extraHtml:
Dedicated subnets per service provide security isolation while shared NAT Gateway or Azure Firewall provides centralized outbound internet connectivity and IP management. Single subnet reduces isolation. Services without internet access can't download updates or access external APIs.

Reference: Azure NAT Gateway

questions.promptHtml: A VNet (10.0.0.0/16) peers with another VNet (10.1.0.0/16). Traffic from 10.0.0.0/16 to specific subnet 10.1.10.0/24 must route through a firewall at 10.0.1.4. Which configuration implements this custom routing?

options.label: No configuration, default routing handles all traffic
User-defined route (UDR) with route to 10.1.10.0/24 via 10.0.1.4 appliance
VNet peering routing automatically applies

questions.correctLabel: User-defined route (UDR) with route to 10.1.10.0/24 via 10.0.1.4 appliance
questions.extraHtml:
UDRs override default Azure routing, directing specific traffic through network virtual appliances like firewalls. Default routing uses direct VNet peering without inspection. VNet peering alone doesn't enforce traffic through security appliances.

Reference: User-defined routes

questions.promptHtml: Your organization requires all VNet traffic to on-premises to route through an Azure Firewall for inspection, even VPN/ExpressRoute default routes. Which UDR configuration enforces this traffic flow?

options.label: No route configuration, default routes to on-premises
UDR with 0.0.0.0/0 to firewall and disable BGP route propagation
VNet peering routes only

questions.correctLabel: UDR with 0.0.0.0/0 to firewall and disable BGP route propagation
questions.extraHtml:
Forced tunneling with 0.0.0.0/0 UDR to firewall and disabled BGP propagation prevents default on-premises routes from bypassing firewall. Default routes allow direct VPN/ExpressRoute without inspection. VNet peering doesn't affect on-premises routing.

Reference: Forced tunneling

questions.promptHtml: An application in subnet A (10.0.1.0/24) cannot reach resources in subnet B (10.0.2.0/24) within the same VNet. Network Watcher IP flow verify shows traffic being blocked. Which diagnostic step identifies the root cause?

options.label: Assume routing issue without verification
Check NSG effective security rules on both subnet and NIC
Restart all VMs to fix connectivity

questions.correctLabel: Check NSG effective security rules on both subnet and NIC
questions.extraHtml:
NSG effective security rules show combined result of subnet and NIC-level NSG rules identifying blocking rules. Assumptions miss actual cause. Restarting VMs doesn't address NSG misconfigurations preventing connectivity.

Reference: NSG diagnostics

questions.promptHtml: Your hub-spoke topology requires spoke VNets to learn routes to on-premises via VPN gateway in the hub. Which Azure Route Server configuration enables this dynamic route propagation?

options.label: Static routes in each spoke VNet
Azure Route Server in hub peering BGP routes to spokes
Manual route table updates per spoke

questions.correctLabel: Azure Route Server in hub peering BGP routes to spokes
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Route Server exchanges routes with network virtual appliances and propagates them to peered VNets, enabling dynamic route learning without static configuration. Static routes require manual updates per change. Manual updates don't scale and miss dynamic changes.

Reference: Azure Route Server

questions.promptHtml: A VNet requires outbound internet connectivity for 1,000 VMs with consistent source IP for external firewall allow-listing. Azure Load Balancer standard already deployed. Which additional component provides outbound SNAT?

options.label: No additional configuration needed
Azure NAT Gateway providing dedicated outbound IPs and SNAT
Individual public IP per VM

questions.correctLabel: Azure NAT Gateway providing dedicated outbound IPs and SNAT
questions.extraHtml:
NAT Gateway provides scalable outbound SNAT with dedicated public IPs for firewall allow-listing without port exhaustion. Load Balancer alone may exhaust SNAT ports. Individual IPs per VM is costly and complex to manage.

Reference: NAT Gateway for outbound

questions.promptHtml: Your organization deploys Azure Virtual Network Manager to centrally manage 50 VNets across subscriptions. Security policy requires blocking traffic between dev and prod VNets while allowing dev-to-dev and prod-to-prod. Which AVNM feature implements this segmentation?

options.label: Manual NSG rules in each VNet
AVNM security admin rules with network groups
No centralized management possible

questions.correctLabel: AVNM security admin rules with network groups
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Virtual Network Manager network groups and security admin rules provide centralized network segmentation policy across VNets. Manual NSG rules per VNet don't scale and lack centralized management. AVNM specifically designed for multi-VNet governance.

Reference: AVNM security

questions.promptHtml: Network Watcher flow logs show high traffic volume from specific source IP to multiple VMs on port 3389. You need to determine if this is legitimate admin traffic or potential attack. Which analysis approach identifies this?

options.label: Ignore traffic analysis
Traffic Analytics with NSG flow logs analyzing patterns and anomalies
Manual log review without tools

questions.correctLabel: Traffic Analytics with NSG flow logs analyzing patterns and anomalies
questions.extraHtml:
Traffic Analytics processes NSG flow logs with ML-based anomaly detection, identifying unusual patterns and potential security issues. Ignoring traffic allows undetected attacks. Manual review doesn't scale or detect subtle patterns.

Reference: Traffic Analytics

questions.promptHtml: Your organization requires DDoS protection for public IP addresses with automatic attack mitigation and access to DDoS rapid response team. Which Azure DDoS protection tier provides these advanced capabilities?

options.label: Basic DDoS protection included free
DDoS Protection Standard with DRR team access
No DDoS protection available

questions.correctLabel: DDoS Protection Standard with DRR team access
questions.extraHtml:
DDoS Protection Standard provides adaptive tuning, attack analytics, mitigation reports, and DDoS Rapid Response team engagement. Basic protection offers only infrastructure-level protection without advanced features. All Azure services have basic protection but advanced scenarios require Standard.

Reference: DDoS Protection Standard

questions.promptHtml: Microsoft Defender for Cloud identifies subnet without NSG as security recommendation. The subnet contains only Azure PaaS services using service endpoints. How should this recommendation be addressed?

options.label: Ignore recommendation for PaaS services
Implement NSG with service tag rules for PaaS services
Remove all resources from subnet

questions.correctLabel: Implement NSG with service tag rules for PaaS services
questions.extraHtml:
NSGs should protect all subnets including PaaS services using service tags for simplified management. Ignoring security recommendations increases risk. Removing resources doesn't address security requirement.

Reference: Service tags in NSGs

questions.promptHtml: Network Watcher Connection Monitor shows latency spikes to on-premises database during business hours. Which diagnostics identify whether issue is Azure networking, VPN tunnel, or on-premises network?

options.label: Assume Azure networking issue without testing
Connection Monitor with multi-hop path analysis and NPM
No diagnostic tools available

questions.correctLabel: Connection Monitor with multi-hop path analysis and NPM
questions.extraHtml:
Connection Monitor with Network Performance Monitor provides hop-by-hop latency analysis isolating issues to specific network segments (Azure, VPN, on-premises). Assumptions don't identify root cause. Azure provides comprehensive network diagnostics.

Reference: Connection Monitor

questions.promptHtml: Your hub VNet has VPN gateway and Azure Firewall. Spoke VNets need to access on-premises via hub gateway and have internet traffic inspected by hub firewall. Which combination achieves both requirements?

options.label: VNet peering with gateway transit and UDRs to firewall for 0.0.0.0/0
No peering configuration
Direct VPN gateway in each spoke

questions.correctLabel: VNet peering with gateway transit and UDRs to firewall for 0.0.0.0/0
questions.extraHtml:
Gateway transit allows spokes to use hub VPN gateway for on-premises connectivity while UDRs route internet traffic (0.0.0.0/0) through firewall for inspection. No peering prevents connectivity. Direct gateways per spoke are costly and complex.

Reference: Gateway transit

questions.promptHtml: A public IP address prefix with 16 addresses (203.0.113.0/28) needs allocation to multiple Application Gateways in different regions. Which IP allocation strategy provides regional distribution?

options.label: Single public IP shared across regions
Create public IPs from prefix and assign by region
Private IPs only

questions.correctLabel: Create public IPs from prefix and assign by region
questions.extraHtml:
Public IP prefix allows creating multiple public IPs from contiguous range, assigning them to resources by region while maintaining IP range continuity. Single IP doesn't provide regional distribution. Public IPs required for internet-facing gateways.

Reference: Public IP prefix

questions.promptHtml: Your organization connects 200 branch offices to Azure using site-to-site VPN with BGP. Route advertisements from on-premises exceed VPN gateway's route limits. Which routing optimization addresses this scale limitation?

options.label: Disable BGP and use static routes
Implement route summarization aggregating routes
Deploy separate gateway per branch

questions.correctLabel: Implement route summarization aggregating routes
questions.extraHtml:
Route summarization reduces number of advertised routes by aggregating specific routes into summary routes, staying within gateway limits. Disabling BGP loses dynamic routing benefits. Separate gateways per branch is cost-prohibitive and unmanageable.

Reference: BGP with VPN Gateway

questions.promptHtml: A VNet peers with multiple other VNets in hub-spoke topology. You need to change the VNet's address space but deployment succeeds with warning about possible connectivity disruption. What is the risk?

options.label: No risk, changes apply immediately without impact
Overlapping address spaces or routing issues during transition
VNet address space cannot be changed

questions.correctLabel: Overlapping address spaces or routing issues during transition
questions.extraHtml:
Changing VNet address space while peered can temporarily disrupt connectivity if new range overlaps with peered VNets or during route propagation. Changes aren't instant and require validation. Address spaces can be changed but require careful planning.

Reference: VNet address space changes

questions.promptHtml: Azure Policy evaluation shows several VNets lack Customer Managed Keys for VNet encryption. Which remediation addresses this compliance gap?

options.label: Ignore VNet encryption requirement
Enable VNet encryption with Custom Managed Keys per VNet
Encryption not supported for VNets

questions.correctLabel: Enable VNet encryption with Custom Managed Keys per VNet
questions.extraHtml:
VNet encryption with Customer Managed Keys provides additional data protection for virtual network traffic meeting compliance requirements. Ignoring requirements violates policy. VNet encryption is supported and recommended for sensitive workloads.

Reference: VNet encryption

questions.promptHtml: Network Watcher shows packets being dropped between two VMs in different subnets. Next Hop troubleshooting shows correct routing. Which additional diagnostic identifies the blocking point?

options.label: Assume network issue without further diagnostics
IP Flow Verify checking NSG rules at subnet and NIC level
Restart VMs to resolve

questions.correctLabel: IP Flow Verify checking NSG rules at subnet and NIC level
questions.extraHtml:
IP Flow Verify tests whether packet is allowed or denied by NSG rules, showing specific rule blocking traffic. Next Hop shows routing but not security filtering. Assumptions and restarts don't identify NSG misconfigurations.

Reference: IP Flow Verify

questions.promptHtml: Your organization implements Azure Bastion requiring dedicated subnet. The Bastion subnet cannot have NSG associated. Which subnet name and minimum size are required?

options.label: Any subnet name with /27 or larger
Subnet named AzureBastionSubnet with /26 or larger
No dedicated subnet required

questions.correctLabel: Subnet named AzureBastionSubnet with /26 or larger
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Bastion requires dedicated subnet named AzureBastionSubnet with minimum /26 CIDR. NSG can be associated but not required. Incorrect naming or sizing prevents Bastion deployment.

Reference: Bastion subnet requirements

questions.promptHtml: An on-premises datacenter requires site-to-site VPN to Azure with 99.9% uptime SLA and automatic failover during gateway maintenance. Which VPN gateway configuration achieves this availability?

options.label: Single VPN gateway basic SKU
Active-active VPN gateway with two tunnels
Single tunnel with manual failover

questions.correctLabel: Active-active VPN gateway with two tunnels
questions.extraHtml:
Active-active VPN gateway configuration provides redundancy with automatic failover during maintenance or failures meeting 99.9% SLA. Basic SKU doesn't support active-active. Single tunnel lacks redundancy.

Reference: Highly available VPN

questions.promptHtml: Your site-to-site VPN requires policy-based IPsec for compatibility with legacy on-premises firewall. Which VPN gateway SKU supports policy-based VPN?

options.label: Standard or HighPerformance SKUs
Basic SKU for policy-based VPN
VpnGw1 or higher

questions.correctLabel: Basic SKU for policy-based VPN
questions.extraHtml:
Basic SKU supports policy-based VPN for legacy device compatibility while standard/HighPerformance and VpnGw SKUs support route-based VPN with more features. Policy-based required when legacy devices don't support route-based.

Reference: VPN Gateway SKUs

questions.promptHtml: A Point-to-Site VPN requires authentication using on-premises Active Directory credentials via RADIUS without Microsoft Entra ID. Which P2S tunnel type and authentication method supports this?

options.label: SSTP tunnel with Microsoft Entra authentication
OpenVPN or IKEv2 tunnel with RADIUS authentication
Azure certificate authentication only

questions.correctLabel: OpenVPN or IKEv2 tunnel with RADIUS authentication
questions.extraHtml:
OpenVPN and IKEv2 support RADIUS authentication integrating with on-premises AD via NPS. SSTP supports RADIUS but less flexible than OpenVPN. Certificate authentication doesn't integrate with AD credentials.

Reference: P2S authentication methods

questions.promptHtml: Remote users require Always On VPN with automatic connection when connected to corporate network. Which Azure P2S VPN feature and client configuration enables this?

options.label: Manual VPN connection requiring user initiation
Always On VPN profile with device tunnel and traffic filters
Site-to-site VPN for users

questions.correctLabel: Always On VPN profile with device tunnel and traffic filters
questions.extraHtml:
Always On VPN configures automatic connection with device tunnel for pre-login connectivity and user tunnel for authenticated access, using traffic filters for selective routing. Manual connection doesn't provide automatic connectivity. S2S VPN connects sites, not individual users.

Reference: Always On VPN

questions.promptHtml: Your organization requires ExpressRoute with 10 Gbps bandwidth and 99.95% SLA. Standard ExpressRoute tier maximum is 10 Gbps but you need higher throughput capacity. Which ExpressRoute option provides 100 Gbps capacity?

options.label: Multiple standard ExpressRoute circuits
ExpressRoute Direct with 100 Gbps port
ExpressRoute is limited to 10 Gbps

questions.correctLabel: ExpressRoute Direct with 100 Gbps port
questions.extraHtml:
ExpressRoute Direct provides dedicated 10 Gbps or 100 Gbps physical ports directly connected to Microsoft network, bypassing standard circuit limitations. Multiple standard circuits don't provide single high-bandwidth connection. ExpressRoute supports up to 100 Gbps via Direct.

Reference: ExpressRoute Direct

questions.promptHtml: An ExpressRoute circuit connects to Azure via private peering. You need to access Microsoft 365 and Dynamics 365 over same circuit without internet breakout. Which peering type enables Microsoft cloud service access?

options.label: Private peering only can access all Microsoft services
Microsoft peering for Microsoft 365 and public cloud services
No solution, internet required

questions.correctLabel: Microsoft peering for Microsoft 365 and public cloud services
questions.extraHtml:
Microsoft peering enables access to Microsoft public cloud services (M365, Dynamics 365, Azure public IPs) over ExpressRoute. Private peering only accesses Azure private IP space. Both peerings can coexist on single circuit.

Reference: ExpressRoute peerings

questions.promptHtml: Your ExpressRoute circuit requires connecting VNets across different Azure regions to on-premises with optimal latency. Which ExpressRoute feature enables cross-region connectivity without multiple circuits?

options.label: Single ExpressRoute circuit limited to one region
ExpressRoute Global Reach connecting remote locations
Multiple ExpressRoute circuits required per region

questions.correctLabel: ExpressRoute Global Reach connecting remote locations
questions.extraHtml:
ExpressRoute Global Reach connects circuits in different regions enabling private connectivity between remote locations through Microsoft global network. Single circuit can connect multiple regional VNets. Multiple circuits can also work but Global Reach optimizes multi-region scenarios.

Reference: ExpressRoute Global Reach

questions.promptHtml: An application requires ultra-low latency to Azure VMs over ExpressRoute. Standard ExpressRoute routing adds slight latency. Which feature bypasses ExpressRoute gateway reducing latency?

options.label: ExpressRoute standard routing through gateway
ExpressRoute FastPath bypassing gateway
Latency cannot be reduced further

questions.correctLabel: ExpressRoute FastPath bypassing gateway
questions.extraHtml:
FastPath sends data traffic directly to VMs bypassing ExpressRoute gateway, reducing latency for performance-critical workloads. Standard routing through gateway adds slight latency. FastPath optimizes data path while control plane still uses gateway.

Reference: ExpressRoute FastPath

questions.promptHtml: ExpressRoute circuit requires encrypting traffic between on-premises and Azure over private peering. Which encryption method secures ExpressRoute private peering traffic?

options.label: ExpressRoute native encryption not supported
IPsec VPN over ExpressRoute private peering (MACsec for link layer)
Encryption not needed for private peering

questions.correctLabel: IPsec VPN over ExpressRoute private peering (MACsec for link layer)
questions.extraHtml:
IPsec over ExpressRoute or MACsec for ExpressRoute Direct encrypts traffic over private peering. ExpressRoute itself doesn't encrypt by default. Private peering alone doesn't guarantee encryption meeting security requirements.

Reference: ExpressRoute encryption

questions.promptHtml: An ExpressRoute connection shows intermittent packet loss. Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) should monitor link health and detect failures faster. How is BFD implemented on ExpressRoute?

options.label: BFD not supported on ExpressRoute
BFD automatically enabled on ExpressRoute circuits
Manual BFD configuration required

questions.correctLabel: BFD automatically enabled on ExpressRoute circuits
questions.extraHtml:
BFD is automatically enabled on ExpressRoute private peering providing fast failure detection (sub-second). No manual configuration needed. BFD improves convergence time during network failures.

Reference: ExpressRoute BFD

questions.promptHtml: Your organization deploys Azure Virtual WAN connecting 50 branch offices and 10 VNets across regions. Which Virtual WAN SKU provides global transit connectivity with inter-hub routing?

options.label: Basic Virtual WAN limited to single region
Standard Virtual WAN with global transit architecture
Virtual WAN doesn't support multiple regions

questions.correctLabel: Standard Virtual WAN with global transit architecture
questions.extraHtml:
Standard Virtual WAN enables global transit architecture with any-to-any connectivity across hubs, VNets, and branches. Basic Virtual WAN supports only site-to-site VPN without inter-hub routing. Virtual WAN specifically designed for multi-region scenarios.

Reference: Virtual WAN SKUs

questions.promptHtml: A Virtual WAN hub requires VPN gateway supporting 1,000 site-to-site connections with 20 Gbps aggregate throughput. Which scale unit configuration meets these requirements?

options.label: 1 scale unit (500 Mbps)
40 scale units (20 Gbps aggregate)
Scale units don't affect throughput

questions.correctLabel: 40 scale units (20 Gbps aggregate)
questions.extraHtml:
Virtual WAN VPN gateway scale units determine aggregate throughput (500 Mbps per unit). 20 Gbps requires 40 scale units. Connection count supported depends on scale units. Scale units directly impact gateway capacity and cost.

Reference: Virtual WAN gateway scale

questions.promptHtml: Virtual WAN hub requires integrating third-party network virtual appliance for traffic inspection. Which Virtual WAN feature enables NVA integration within hub?

options.label: NVA cannot integrate with Virtual WAN
Virtual WAN NVA integration routing traffic through third-party appliance
Separate VNet with NVA only

questions.correctLabel: Virtual WAN NVA integration routing traffic through third-party appliance
questions.extraHtml:
Virtual WAN supports third-party NVA deployment in hub with routing integration enabling traffic inspection for hub-connected resources. NVAs fully integrate with Virtual WAN. Separate VNet defeats centralized Virtual WAN architecture.

Reference: NVA in Virtual WAN

questions.promptHtml: Your organization connects 4 ExpressRoute circuits in different regions. Each circuit is 10 Gbps. One circuit fails. Which routing configuration ensures traffic automatically uses remaining circuits without manual intervention?

options.label: Static routes requiring manual failover
BGP with AS path prepending or local preference for automatic failover
No redundancy configuration possible

questions.correctLabel: BGP with AS path prepending or local preference for automatic failover
questions.extraHtml:
BGP dynamically reroutes traffic through available paths using path attributes (AS path prepending, local preference, MED) providing automatic failover without manual intervention. Static routes require manual updates. ExpressRoute BGP enables resilient multi-circuit deployments.

Reference: ExpressRoute redundancy

questions.promptHtml: A virtual network gateway for site-to-site VPN shows BGP peer status as disconnected. Diagnosis reveals BGP ASN mismatch between Azure and on-premises. What ASN range is valid for Azure VPN Gateway custom ASN?

options.label: Only Azure default ASN 65515
Valid BGP ASN ranges 64512-65534 (private) or registered public ASN
Any 32-bit number

questions.correctLabel: Valid BGP ASN ranges 64512-65534 (private) or registered public ASN
questions.extraHtml:
Azure VPN Gateway supports custom BGP ASN using private ASN ranges (64512-65534) or registered public ASN. Reserved ASNs (65515, 65517) used by Azure. 32-bit ASN requires specific configuration.

Reference: BGP ASN requirements

questions.promptHtml: Point-to-Site VPN users report intermittent disconnections every 12 hours requiring manual reconnection. Which P2S setting adjustment extends session duration?

options.label: Default session timeout cannot be adjusted
Configure extended idle timeout in VPN gateway P2S configuration
Increase VPN gateway SKU

questions.correctLabel: Configure extended idle timeout in VPN gateway P2S configuration
questions.extraHtml:
P2S VPN idle timeout configuration extends session duration before automatic disconnection. Default timeout may be too short for long-running sessions. SKU doesn't affect session timeouts.

Reference: P2S configuration

questions.promptHtml: An ExpressRoute circuit advertises 200+ routes from on-premises exceeding Azure VNet route limits. Which route optimization prevents route limit issues while maintaining connectivity?

options.label: Random route removal
Route summarization aggregating specific routes into summary routes
Disable BGP route advertisement

questions.correctLabel: Route summarization aggregating specific routes into summary routes
questions.extraHtml:
Route summarization reduces advertised route count by aggregating specific prefixes into summary routes fitting within Azure limits without losing connectivity. Random removal creates connectivity issues. Disabling BGP breaks dynamic routing.

Reference: ExpressRoute route optimization

questions.promptHtml: A multi-tier application requires distributing traffic across 50 backend VMs with session persistence to same VM for 10-minute duration. Which Load Balancer configuration achieves this?

options.label: Default 5-tuple hash distribution
Source IP affinity distribution mode with client IP persistence
No session persistence possible

questions.correctLabel: Source IP affinity distribution mode with client IP persistence
questions.extraHtml:
Source IP affinity (session affinity) uses 2-tuple (source IP, destination IP) or 3-tuple (source IP, destination IP, protocol) hash ensuring same client connects to same backend maintaining session stickiness. Default 5-tuple may route sessions to different backends. Load Balancer supports multiple distribution modes.

Reference: Load Balancer distribution modes

questions.promptHtml: Your organization requires internal Load Balancer for private applications but also needs outbound internet access for backend VMs. Which configuration provides both internal load balancing and outbound connectivity?

options.label: Internal LB alone provides outbound connectivity
Internal LB with separate NAT Gateway or outbound rules
Public LB required for any outbound connectivity

questions.correctLabel: Internal LB with separate NAT Gateway or outbound rules
questions.extraHtml:
Internal Load Balancer doesn't provide outbound connectivity by default. NAT Gateway or explicit outbound rules on standard LB enable outbound internet access. Internal LB lacks public IPs for outbound. Multiple LBs (internal and public) or NAT Gateway handle both scenarios.

Reference: Outbound connections

questions.promptHtml: A Load Balancer requires forwarding specific source IPs to specific backend VMs for admin access (port 3389) independent of load balancing rules. Which LB feature enables this static port forwarding?

options.label: Load balancing rules don't support specific IPs
Inbound NAT rules mapping source IP/port to specific backend
Outbound rules configure inbound traffic

questions.correctLabel: Inbound NAT rules mapping source IP/port to specific backend
questions.extraHtml:
Inbound NAT rules map specific frontend IP and port to specific backend VM enabling direct connections like RDP or SSH independent from load balancing distribution. Load balancing rules distribute to all backend pool members. Outbound rules are for outbound, not inbound traffic.

Reference: Inbound NAT rules

questions.promptHtml: Traffic Manager profiles require routing users to nearest healthy endpoint by latency. One endpoint becomes unhealthy. Which Traffic Manager probe frequency ensures fastest failover detection?

options.label: 30-second probe interval (default)
Fast endpoint monitoring with 10-second interval
No health monitoring needed

questions.correctLabel: Fast endpoint monitoring with 10-second interval
questions.extraHtml:
Fast endpoint monitoring (10 seconds) provides quicker health detection and faster failover than standard 30-second interval, critical for latency-sensitive applications. Default 30-second may be too slow. Traffic Manager requires health probes for automatic failover.

Reference: Traffic Manager monitoring

questions.promptHtml: Virtual machines behind Gateway Load Balancer require traffic inspection by network virtual appliance before reaching actual backend. Which load balancing pattern enables this transparent traffic inspection?

options.label: Standard Load Balancer without NVA integration
Gateway Load Balancer chaining traffic through NVA
Application Gateway Layer 7 inspection

questions.correctLabel: Gateway Load Balancer chaining traffic through NVA
questions.extraHtml:
Gateway Load Balancer transparently inserts network virtual appliances (firewalls, IDS/IPS) in traffic path enabling traffic inspection while maintaining connection to backend pools. Standard LB doesn't provide NVA chaining. App Gateway provides Layer 7 WAF but not arbitrary NVA insertion.

Reference: Gateway Load Balancer

questions.promptHtml: Application Gateway requires auto-scaling between 2 and 100 compute units based on traffic. Which Application Gateway SKU supports autoscaling?

options.label: Standard_v1 or WAF_v1 SKU
Standard_v2 or WAF_v2 SKU with autoscaling configuration
Autoscaling not supported

questions.correctLabel: Standard_v2 or WAF_v2 SKU with autoscaling configuration
questions.extraHtml:
Application Gateway v2 SKU (Standard_v2/WAF_v2) supports autoscaling and zone redundancy. V1 SKU requires manual capacity management. Autoscaling adjusts capacity based on metrics without manual intervention.

Reference: Application Gateway v2

questions.promptHtml: Application Gateway backend pool includes VMs in multiple subnets with different HTTP settings. One backend requires 60-second timeout while others need 20 seconds. How are different HTTP settings applied per backend?

options.label: Single HTTP setting applies to all backends
Create multiple HTTP settings and routing rules per backend requirement
HTTP settings cannot differ per backend

questions.correctLabel: Create multiple HTTP settings and routing rules per backend requirement
questions.extraHtml:
Application Gateway routing rules associate listeners with backend pools using specific HTTP settings, enabling different timeout, cookie, and probe configurations per backend. Single HTTP setting doesn't provide flexibility. Multiple HTTP settings supported per gateway.

Reference: HTTP settings

questions.promptHtml: Application Gateway requires URL-based routing where /api/* routes to API backend pool and /images/* routes to image backend pool. Which Application Gateway feature implements this path-based routing?

options.label: Basic routing without path awareness
Path-based routing rules with URL path maps
Host-based routing only

questions.correctLabel: Path-based routing rules with URL path maps
questions.extraHtml:
Path-based routing rules with URL path maps route requests to different backend pools based on URL path patterns enabling microservices architecture routing. Basic routing lacks path awareness. Host-based routing uses hostnames, not paths.

Reference: URL path-based routing

questions.promptHtml: Application Gateway health probes show backends unhealthy despite VMs responding to direct HTTP requests. Custom health probe configured but probe URL returns 404. What is the likely cause?

options.label: Application Gateway malfunction
Health probe URL path doesn't exist or returns incorrect status code
VMs actually unhealthy

questions.correctLabel: Health probe URL path doesn't exist or returns incorrect status code within expected range
questions.extraHtml:
Health probes require backend responding with 200-399 status code range at specified path. 404 indicates probe path doesn't exist. Direct HTTP requests to different paths may succeed. Probe configuration must match actual backend health endpoint.

Reference: Application Gateway health probes

questions.promptHtml: Azure Front Door requires end-to-end TLS encryption from client to origin backend. Client connects via HTTPS to Front Door. How do you ensure Front Door to origin also uses HTTPS?

options.label: Front Door to origin automatically uses HTTPS
Configure origin with HTTPS protocol and enforce minimum TLS version
TLS termination at Front Door only

questions.correctLabel: Configure origin with HTTPS protocol and enforce minimum TLS version
questions.extraHtml:
End-to-end TLS requires configuring origin with HTTPS protocol ensuring encrypted connection from Front Door to backend. Front Door doesn't automatically enforce origin HTTPS. TLS termination-only decrypts at Front Door sending plaintext to origin.

Reference: Front Door end-to-end TLS

questions.promptHtml: Azure Front Door caches static content but dynamic API responses are being cached causing stale data. Which Front Door configuration granularly controls caching behavior per route?

options.label: Global caching cannot be adjusted per route
Route rules with caching disabled for dynamic content and enabled for static
Disable caching entirely

questions.correctLabel: Route rules with caching disabled for dynamic content and enabled for static
questions.extraHtml:
Front Door route rules enable per-route caching configuration with query string caching behavior, allowing static content caching while bypassing cache for dynamic APIs. Global settings lack granularity. Disabling caching entirely removes performance benefits for static content.

Reference: Front Door caching

questions.promptHtml: Front Door origin group requires fastest origin selection based on real-time latency measurements. Which routing method achieves this lowest-latency routing?

options.label: Priority-based routing to primary origin
Latency-based routing with latency sensitivity measurements
Weighted routing with equal distribution

questions.correctLabel: Latency-based routing with latency sensitivity measurements
questions.extraHtml:
Latency-based routing measures latency to origins in real-time selecting fastest response origin. Priority routing doesn't consider performance. Weighted routing distributes without performance optimization.

Reference: Front Door routing methods

questions.promptHtml: Azure Front Door URL rewrite required to transform incoming URL /old-path/* to /new-path/* before forwarding to origin. Which Front Door feature implements URL transformation?

options.label: Routing rules without transformation
Rules Engine with URL rewrite actions
URL transformation not supported

questions.correctLabel: Rules Engine with URL rewrite actions
questions.extraHtml:
Front Door Rules Engine enables URL rewrite and redirect actions transforming URLs, modifying headers, and implementing custom routing logic. Basic routing doesn't transform URLs. Front Door provides comprehensive traffic manipulation.

Reference: Front Door Rules Engine

questions.promptHtml: A PaaS service (Azure SQL Database) require private connectivity from specific VNet without public endpoint exposure. Which Azure networking feature provides this private connectivity?

options.label: Public endpoint with firewall rules
Azure Private Endpoint with private IP in VNet
Service endpoints with VNet binding

questions.correctLabel: Azure Private Endpoint with private IP in VNet
questions.extraHtml:
Private Endpoint assigns private IP address from VNet to Azure service, eliminating public endpoint exposure and enabling private connectivity. Public endpoints with firewall still expose service to internet. Service endpoints secure traffic but service remains on public IP.

Reference: Private Endpoint

questions.promptHtml: Multiple workloads in different VNets require private endpoints to Azure Storage. Each VNet has Private DNS zone for storage private endpoint resolution. How should DNS be architected for consistent resolution?

options.label: Separate DNS zones per VNet creating management complexity
Centralized Private DNS zone linked to all VNets with private endpoint
No DNS configuration needed

questions.correctLabel: Centralized Private DNS zone linked to all VNets with private endpoint
questions.extraHtml:
Hub Private DNS zone linked to spoke VNets provides centralized DNS management for private endpoint resolution across VNets. Separate zones per VNet multiply management overhead. Private endpoints require DNS to resolve private IPs.

Reference: Private Endpoint DNS

questions.promptHtml: Your organization exposes self-hosted API as Private Link Service allowing customers to consume via Private Endpoint. Which Standard Load Balancer configuration is required?

options.label: No load balancer required
Internal Standard Load Balancer fronting service backend
Public Load Balancer exposed to internet

questions.correctLabel: Internal Standard Load Balancer fronting service backend
questions.extraHtml:
Private Link Service requires Internal Standard Load Balancer fronting workload to expose via Private Link. No load balancer prevents Private Link creation. Public load balancer defeats private access purpose.

Reference: Private Link Service

questions.promptHtml: On-premises clients require private connectivity to Azure service via Private Link. ExpressRoute connects on-premises to Azure. What additional configuration enables on-premises to resolve private endpoint private IPs?

options.label: On-premises automatically resolves private endpoints
DNS forwarding from on-premises to Azure DNS Private Resolver or DNS servers
No DNS configuration needed

questions.correctLabel: DNS forwarding from on-premises to Azure DNS Private Resolver or DNS servers
questions.extraHtml:
On-premises DNS must forward Azure service FQDNs to Azure DNS servers or DNS Private Resolver to resolve private endpoint IPs. Automatic resolution doesn't work without forwarding. Private DNS resolution requires proper DNS configuration.

Reference: On-premises Private Endpoint DNS

questions.promptHtml: VNet requires restricting Azure Storage access only to specific subnet while blocking other subnets. Which feature provides subnet-level service access control?

options.label: Network Security Groups (NSGs) blocking storage IPs
Service Endpoint with VNet rules on storage account
No subnet-level control available

questions.correctLabel: Service Endpoint with VNet rules on storage account
questions.extraHtml:
Service Endpoints enable Azure PaaS services to restrict access to specific VNet subnets using VNet rules. NSGs can't effectively filter Azure PaaS services using service tags. Services support subnet-level access control through service endpoints.

Reference: Service Endpoints

questions.promptHtml: Azure Storage account requires service endpoint policy enforcing connections only to approved storage accounts, blocking other storage accounts. Which feature provides this service-level filtering?

options.label: Service endpoints without policy control
Service Endpoint Policy with allowed resource list
Service policy not supported

questions.correctLabel: Service Endpoint Policy with allowed resource list
questions.extraHtml:
Service Endpoint Policies provide additional control over service endpoints, allowing or denying specific Azure service resources. Basic service endpoints enable VNet-level access without resource-level filtering. Policies add granular control.

Reference: Service Endpoint Policies

questions.promptHtml: Your architecture requires private endpoint for Azure SQL and service endpoint for Azure Storage in same subnet. Can both features coexist in single subnet?

options.label: Only one feature per subnet
Private endpoints and service endpoints coexist in same subnet
Separate VNets required per feature

questions.correctLabel: Private endpoints and service endpoints coexist in same subnet
questions.extraHtml:
Private Endpoints and Service Endpoints are complementary and coexist in same subnet. Different features for different services or different access patterns. Multiple private connectivity options available simultaneously.

Reference: Service endpoint limitations

questions.promptHtml: A private endpoint connects to Azure SQL Database. On-premises users via VPN report cannot resolve SQL FQDN to private IP. What DNS configuration resolves this?

options.label: On-premises users automatically resolve private IPs
Azure Private DNS zone linked to VNet with conditional forwarding from on-premises
No solution for on-premises access

questions.correctLabel: Azure Private DNS zone linked to VNet with conditional forwarding from on-premises
questions.extraHtml:
Private DNS zone linked to VNet contains A record for private endpoint. On-premises DNS forwards queries for Azure service FQDNs to Azure DNS Private Resolver or VNet DNS servers. Automatic resolution doesn't work without forwarding configuration.

Reference: Private DNS integration

questions.promptHtml: Azure Storage account with service endpoint receives connection attempts from internet despite VNet rules configured. What additional configuration prevents public internet access?

options.label: Service endpoints alone block public access
Disable public network access or configure firewall rules denying internet
Public access cannot be prevented

questions.correctLabel: Disable public network access or configure firewall rules denying internet
questions.extraHtml:
Service endpoints secure traffic from VNet but don't disable public endpoint. Disabling public network access or firewall rules blocking internet prevent public access. Service endpoints are one layer of defense requiring additional configuration for complete isolation.

Reference: Storage network security

questions.promptHtml: Private Link Service exposes internal application to customers via Private Endpoint. Which IP addressing consideration affects Private Link Service design?

options.label: Private Link Service uses public IPs
NAT IP address configuration for Private Link Service
No IP addressing considerations

questions.correctLabel: NAT IP address configuration for Private Link Service
questions.extraHtml:
Private Link Service requires NAT IP addresses from subnet for source NAT providing stable IPs for consumer private endpoint connections. Private Link uses private IPs. NAT configuration critical for proper service exposure.

Reference: Private Link NAT IPs

questions.promptHtml: VNet with multiple subnets requires granular service access where Subnet A can access Azure Storage while Subnet B cannot. How is this subnet-level control implemented?

options.label: Single service endpoint applies to entire VNet
Enable service endpoint on specific subnets and configure VNet rules per subnet
No granular subnet control available

questions.correctLabel: Enable service endpoint on specific subnets and configure VNet rules per subnet
questions.extraHtml:
Service endpoints are enabled per subnet with VNet rules on Azure services specifying allowed subnets, providing granular control. Service endpoints don't automatically apply to entire VNet. Subnet-level enablement combined with service VNet rules achieves granular access control.

Reference: Service endpoint configuration

questions.promptHtml: Private Endpoint for Azure SQL Database requires DNS resolution. Multiple private endpoints exist for different SQL servers. How does DNS resolve correct private IP per SQL server?

options.label: All private endpoints share same private IP
Private DNS zone A records map FQDN to specific private endpoint IP
DNS cannot distinguish between private endpoints

questions.correctLabel: Private DNS zone A records map FQDN to specific private endpoint IP
questions.extraHtml:
Each private endpoint receives unique private IP with DNS A record mapping service FQDN to that specific private IP. Multiple private endpoints create multiple DNS records. DNS resolution is specific per service instance.

Reference: Private Endpoint DNS records

questions.promptHtml: A subnet (10.0.1.0/24) requires Network Security Group allowing HTTP (80) from internet and RDP (3389) from corporate IP 203.0.113.0/24. All other traffic denied. Which NSG rules implement this?

options.label: No NSG rules needed, default allows all
Allow rules for HTTP from Internet service tag and RDP from 203.0.113.0/24, deny all at lower priority
Deny rules only

questions.correctLabel: Allow rules for HTTP from Internet service tag and RDP from 203.0.113.0/24, deny all at lower priority
questions.extraHtml:
NSG rules processed by priority with specific allow rules (HTTP from Internet, RDP from source IP) taking precedence before default deny-all. Default NSG rules deny internet inbound. Explicit rules required for allowed traffic.

Reference: NSG rule processing

questions.promptHtml: Application tier VMs (10.0.2.0/24) require connectivity to database tier (10.0.3.0/24) on port 1433. Both tiers in same VNet. How should security be implemented while minimizing NSG rules?

options.label: No NSG rules needed for same-VNet traffic
Application Security Groups (ASGs) for app and database tiers with NSG rules between ASGs
Individual IP-based NSG rules per VM

questions.correctLabel: Application Security Groups (ASGs) for app and database tiers with NSG rules between ASGs
questions.extraHtml:
ASGs group VMs logically enabling NSG rules based on application role instead of IP addresses, simplifying rule management especially with dynamic VMs. Same-VNet traffic isn't automatically secured. IP-based rules per VM don't scale.

Reference: Application Security Groups

questions.promptHtml: NSG flow logs show high traffic volume but you need to understand traffic patterns, top talkers, and anomalous flows. Which Azure feature analyzes NSG flow logs for insights?

options.label: Manual flow log analysis
Traffic Analytics with AI-powered pattern recognition
No flow log analysis available

questions.correctLabel: Traffic Analytics with AI-powered pattern recognition
questions.extraHtml:
Traffic Analytics processes NSG flow logs providing visualizations, anomaly detection, top talkers analysis, and security insights. Manual analysis doesn't scale or detect patterns. Azure provides built-in flow log analytics.

Reference: Traffic Analytics

questions.promptHtml: Azure Firewall requires centralized management of firewall policies across 20 Azure Firewalls in different regions and subscriptions. Which feature enables centralized policy management?

options.label: Manual configuration per firewall
Azure Firewall Manager with hierarchical policies
No centralized management available

questions.correctLabel: Azure Firewall Manager with hierarchical policies
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Firewall Manager provides centralized management with hierarchical policies inheriting base policies while allowing region-specific customization. Manual per-firewall configuration doesn't scale. Firewall Manager specifically designed for multi-firewall governance.

Reference: Azure Firewall Manager

questions.promptHtml: Azure Firewall requires URL filtering based on FQDN for outbound HTTPS traffic instead of IP-based filtering. Which Azure Firewall rule type enables FQDN-based filtering?

options.label: Network rules with IP addresses only
Application rules with FQDN filtering and TLS inspection
FQDN filtering not supported

questions.correctLabel: Application rules with FQDN filtering and TLS inspection
questions.extraHtml:
Application rules support FQDN filtering with TLS inspection for HTTPS traffic, handling dynamic IPs behind domain names. Network rules support IP/port but not FQDNs. FQDN filtering critical for cloud services with changing IPs.

Reference: Azure Firewall FQDN filtering

questions.promptHtml: Azure Firewall protects spoke VNets in hub-spoke topology. Which Azure Firewall SKU provides intrusion detection and prevention system (IDPS) capabilities?

options.label: Azure Firewall Basic SKU
Azure Firewall Premium with IDPS
Standard Firewall with IDPS

questions.correctLabel: Azure Firewall Premium with IDPS
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Firewall Premium provides advanced threat protection including IDPS, TLS inspection, URL filtering, and web categories. Standard firewall lacks advanced security features. Premium SKU required for comprehensive threat protection.

Reference: Azure Firewall Premium

questions.promptHtml: Virtual WAN hub requires Azure Firewall for secured hub functionality. Which deployment pattern integrates Azure Firewall with Virtual WAN hub?

options.label: Separate VNet with firewall without hub integration
Deploy Azure Firewall inside Virtual WAN hub creating secure hub
Firewall cannot integrate with Virtual WAN

questions.correctLabel: Deploy Azure Firewall inside Virtual WAN hub creating secure hub
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Firewall deploys directly in Virtual WAN hub creating secured hub with centralized security policy for all hub-connected resources. Separate VNet defeats centralized Virtual WAN architecture. Firewall integrates with Virtual WAN providing security services.

Reference: Secured Virtual Hub

questions.promptHtml: Web Application Firewall on Application Gateway shows false positives blocking legitimate traffic. Which WAF configuration allows tuning rules without disabling WAF entirely?

options.label: Disable WAF for all traffic
WAF exclusion lists and custom rules to fine-tune detection
WAF rules cannot be adjusted

questions.correctLabel: WAF exclusion lists and custom rules to fine-tune detection
questions.extraHtml:
WAF supports exclusion lists removing specific request attributes from inspection and custom rules overriding managed rule set behavior, reducing false positives while maintaining protection. Disabling WAF removes security. WAF provides extensive tuning capabilities.

Reference: WAF tuning

questions.promptHtml: Azure Front Door Web Application Firewall requires bot protection and rate limiting per client IP. Which WAF capability provides these advanced protections?

options.label: Standard WAF managed rules only
WAF custom rules with rate limiting and bot manager rule set
Bot protection not available

questions.correctLabel: WAF custom rules with rate limiting and bot manager rule set
questions.extraHtml:
WAF custom rules enable rate limiting per client IP and bot manager rule sets provide bot detection and mitigation. Standard managed rules provide OWASP protection. Front Door WAF offers advanced protection capabilities.

Reference: WAF custom rules

questions.promptHtml: Multiple Application Gateways across regions require consistent WAF policy. Which WAF deployment approach enables policy consistency?

options.label: Manual policy configuration per gateway
WAF policy resource associated with multiple Application Gateways
No policy sharing mechanism

questions.correctLabel: WAF policy resource associated with multiple Application Gateways
questions.extraHtml:
WAF policy is standalone resource associable with multiple Application Gateways ensuring consistent protection across deployments. Manual per-gateway configuration creates policy drift. WAF policies provide centralized management.

Reference: WAF policy

questions.promptHtml: NSG flow logs required for network forensics and security analysis. Where are NSG flow log data stored and how long retained?

options.label: Flow logs stored only in NSG without external storage
Flow logs stored in Azure Storage account with configurable retention
No flow log storage or retention

questions.correctLabel: Flow logs stored in Azure Storage account with configurable retention
questions.extraHtml:
NSG flow logs stored in Azure Storage account with configured retention period (1-365 days) for long-term analysis. Logs not stored in NSG itself. Storage and retention configuration required for flow log enablement.

Reference: NSG flow logs

questions.promptHtml: Azure Firewall processes outbound traffic from 10.0.0.0/16 to internet via SNAT. Destination services block Azure Firewall's public IP. Which firewall configuration provides predictable SNAT IPs for allow-listing?

options.label: Firewall uses random Azure IPs
Explicit outbound SNAT IP configuration with Public IP prefix
SNAT IP cannot be controlled

questions.correctLabel: Explicit outbound SNAT IP configuration with Public IP prefix
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Firewall supports explicit SNAT IP configuration using Public IP prefix providing predictable outbound IPs for destination allow-lists. Default uses available firewall IPs. Public IP prefix enables IP range management.

Reference: Azure Firewall SNAT

questions.promptHtml: WAF on Application Gateway requires CRS 3.2 rule set with specific rules disabled causing false positives. Which WAF configuration supports rule set customization?

options.label: Managed rule sets cannot be customized
Rule set with individual rule disablement and exclusions
Only custom rules, no managed rules

questions.correctLabel: Rule set with individual rule disablement and exclusions
questions.extraHtml:
Managed rule sets support per-rule disablement and exclusions allowing fine-tuning while maintaining overall protection. Managed rules customizable. Mix of managed and custom rules provides balanced protection.

Reference: WAF CRS customization

questions.promptHtml: Azure Firewall threat intelligence alerts show high confidence malicious IPs but firewall configured in alert-only mode. What change enables firewall to block threat intel-identified threats?

options.label: Alert mode only, no blocking available
Enable threat intelligence in Alert and Deny mode
Disable threat intelligence

questions.correctLabel: Enable threat intelligence in Alert and Deny mode
questions.extraHtml:
Threat intelligence supports Alert Only (logging) or Alert and Deny (logging + blocking) modes. Alert mode provides visibility without enforcement. Deny mode blocks malicious IPs/FQDNs based on Microsoft threat intelligence.

Reference: Firewall threat intelligence

questions.promptHtml: Network traffic between Azure regions must remain on Microsoft backbone without traversing public internet. Which networking feature ensures traffic stays on Microsoft network?

options.label: Public internet routing between regions
Azure global network with ExpressRoute Global Reach or VNet peering
No private inter-region connectivity

questions.correctLabel: Azure global network with ExpressRoute Global Reach or VNet peering
questions.extraHtml:
Azure global backbone network carries VNet-to-VNet traffic between regions without public internet exposure. ExpressRoute Global Reach extends private connectivity. Inter-region traffic automatically uses Microsoft network.

Reference: Azure global network

questions.promptHtml: A VNet uses custom DNS servers (10.0.1.4, 10.0.1.5). VMs report cannot resolve Azure-provided names like Azure Storage. What DNS configuration resolves Azure services while using custom DNS?

options.label: Replace custom DNS with Azure DNS only
Custom DNS forward unresolved queries to Azure DNS (168.63.129.16)
Azure names cannot be resolved with custom DNS

questions.correctLabel: Custom DNS forward unresolved queries to Azure DNS (168.63.129.16)
questions.extraHtml:
Custom DNS servers forward queries for Azure services to Azure DNS (168.63.129.16) enabling resolution of Azure-provided names while maintaining custom DNS for organization names. Azure-only DNS loses custom resolution. Coexistence requires forwarding configuration.

Reference: Custom DNS forwarding

questions.promptHtml: ExpressRoute circuit connects to multiple VNets via ExpressRoute gateway. One VNet requires traffic rate limiting to 1 Gbps while others need full circuit bandwidth. How is per-VNet bandwidth controlled?

options.label: ExpressRoute circuit bandwidth equally splits across VNets
Per-VNet bandwidth limits not directly supported; design architecture with traffic management
Per-VNet limit configuration available

questions.correctLabel: Per-VNet bandwidth limits not directly supported; design architecture with traffic management
questions.extraHtml:
ExpressRoute doesn't natively support per-VNet bandwidth allocation. Architecture design using separate circuits, traffic management, or VNet-level prioritization required for bandwidth control. Circuit bandwidth shared across connected VNets.

Reference: ExpressRoute gateway constraints

questions.promptHtml: Virtual network requires multiple address ranges but subnets showing as "failed" when ranges overlap. What is the Azure VNet address space limitation causing this?

options.label: VNets support any overlapping ranges
VNet address spaces must not overlap within VNet or with peered VNets
Only single address range allowed per VNet

questions.correctLabel: VNet address spaces must not overlap within VNet or with peered VNets
questions.extraHtml:
VNet address spaces and peered VNet ranges must not overlap to prevent routing conflicts. Multiple non-overlapping ranges supported per VNet. Overlapping ranges cause deployment failures and routing issues.

Reference: VNet address overlap

questions.promptHtml: Load Balancer backend pool VMs showing intermittent connectivity despite health probes passing. Load balancer logs show SNAT port exhaustion. Which configuration prevents SNAT port exhaustion?

options.label: Increase VM count without addressing SNAT
Add outbound rules with more frontend IPs or implement NAT Gateway
SNAT exhaustion cannot be prevented

questions.correctLabel: Add outbound rules with more frontend IPs or implement NAT Gateway
questions.extraHtml:
SNAT port exhaustion resolved by increasing available SNAT ports through additional frontend IPs on outbound rules or offloading to NAT Gateway providing 64K ports per IP. VM count alone doesn't address SNAT. Azure provides scalable outbound connectivity solutions.

Reference: SNAT exhaustion troubleshooting
